purpose: Breaks down AI productivity transmission into five discrete steps with distinct speeds
components: Step 1: Technology availability (AI models exist and are accessible)
Step 2: Firm adoption (companies deploy AI tools)
Step 3: Task-level gains (individual tasks performed faster/better)
Step 4: Firm-level gains (overall firm productivity improves)
current_assessment: Step 1 is complete, Step 2 is well underway but uneven across sectors, Step 3 is observable in early-adopter tasks, Steps 4-5 are not yet measurable. Anchoring asymmetry: bulls anchor to Step 2 speed, bears anchor to Steps 4-5 lags.
limitations: Assumes sequential progression which may not hold strictly
Each step has different measurement challenges
The anchoring asymmetry critique applies to the framework itself — which step is most informative?
Does not predict speeds, only identifies where bottlenecks exist
